description: Cross-implementation known-answer vectors pinning the task-thread envelope canonicalization (json-stable-v1) and its sha256 digest. `canonical` is the EXACT canonical-JSON string the SDK canonicalEnvelopeJson() produces for `envelope`; `expectedHex` is the lowercase-hex sha256 over those UTF-8 bytes — the on-chain changes_hash / rejection_hash / rationale_hash. The SDK task-thread test and the storefront task-thread test BOTH import canonicalEnvelopeJson/envelopeHash from @tetsuo-ai/marketplace-sdk and assert these SAME values, so any future canonicalization drift in either repo (Unicode handling, undefined-dropping, number/array formatting) fails loudly instead of silently diverging.

source.note: Generated locally (no network, no wallet) from the SDK's own canonicalizer; cross-checked against an independent node:crypto sha256 over the same canonical bytes.

vectors.name: tricky-nested-nonascii-undefined-drop
vectors.note: Top-level keys and nested attachment keys are out of sorted order (canonicalization MUST sort them); non-ASCII + emoji + escaped quotes/angle-brackets in the body (NOT Unicode-normalized); a two-element attachments array; a large ts. Test harnesses MUST also inject an extra `undefined`-valued property before hashing to prove json-stable-v1 drops it without changing the digest.

envelope.body: Café — naïve façade · 日本語 · emoji 🚀 · "quotes" & <tags>

vectors.name: minimal-first-message-empty-attachments
vectors.note: First message in a thread: parentHash null, empty body, empty attachments array, small ts.
